segments.text: At the same time Merodach-baladan, son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah, because he had heard that Hezekiah was sick and had recovered.

Hezekiah happily welcomed the visitors and showed them what he had in his treasury—all the silver, the gold, the spices, and the expensive oils. He also showed them his whole armory and all that he had in his storehouses. In fact there wasn't anything in his palace or in the whole of his kingdom that Hezekiah didn't show them.
Then the prophet Isaiah went to King Hezekiah and asked him, {
Where did those men come from, and what did they tell you?
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: They came to see me from a long way away, from Babylon,
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: Hezekiah replied.
What did they see in your palace?
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Isaiah asked.
They saw everything in my palace,
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: replied Hezekiah.
There wasn't anything in all my storehouses I didn't show them.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Isaiah told Hezekiah, {
Listen to what the Lord says:
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: You can be certain that the time is coming when everything in your palace, and everything that your forefathers have saved up until now, will be taken away to Babylon. There will be nothing left, says the Lord.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Some of your sons, your own offspring, will be taken to serve as eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
segments.speaker: Isaiah

segments.text: Hezekiah said to Isaiah, {
The message from the Lord that you have told me is fine.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah

segments.text: For he said to himself,
There'll definitely be peace and safety in my lifetime.
segments.speaker: Hezekiah
